producer_note: Ichiro's Malt is produced at Chichibu Distillery in Saitama Prefecture, founded by Ichiro Akuto in 2007.
Historic Volnay-based domaine with three 1er cru monopoles in Volnay; also works premier and grand crus across the Cote de Beaune and Cote de Nuits.
Grape-based vodka distilled at Distillerie de Chevanceaux, sourcing Mauzac Blanc and Ugni Blanc from Gaillac in Southwest France.
Loch Lomond Distillery in Alexandria is officially classified as Highland; brand also bottles blended Scotch using whiskies from across Scotland.
Single malt distillery founded 1896 by John Dewar & Sons in Aberfeldy, Perth and Kinross, in the Central Highlands.
Single malt distillery in Brechin, Angus, classified as Eastern Highland; the only distillery in the county of Angus.
Tequila produced at La Rojena distillery in the town of Tequila, Jalisco; oldest active distillery in Latin America (1812).
Single malt distillery in Brechin, Angus; in the Eastern Highland whisky region.
Cuban cigar brand using short filler tobacco from the Vuelta Abajo and Semi Vuelta regions of Pinar del Rio.
Bordeaux negociant and vigneron based in Saint-Emilion; founder of Chateau Valandraud, Premier Grand Cru Classe.
Speyside single malt distillery in Dufftown, Moray, founded 1823; owned by Diageo.
Speyside single malt distillery in Carron, Strathspey, founded 1852; spirit primarily used in Johnnie Walker blends.
Sake brand of Nakao Brewery in Takehara, Hiroshima Prefecture, on the coast of the Seto Inland Sea.
Blended Scotch range from William Grant & Sons combining Highland, Speyside, Lowland grain and Island malts.
Speyside single malt distillery founded 1840 in the town of Rothes, Moray; owned by Campari Group since 2005.
Mezcal sourced from multiple Mexican states including Guerrero, Oaxaca and San Luis Potosi.
Doburoku producer based in Tono City, Iwate Prefecture (designated doburoku special zone), founded 2011 by Yotaro Sasaki.
Independent bottler range (Hunter Laing) of single cask single malts sourced from distilleries across Scotland.
Cognac house in Saint-Sulpice de Cognac sourcing eaux-de-vie from Grande Champagne, Petite Champagne, Borderies and Fins Bois crus.
Italian brandy distillery in Castel Maggiore, Bologna (Emilia-Romagna); brandy from Trebbiano di Romagna.
Negociant arm of Domaine La Soufrandiere working exclusively in the Maconnais (Macon-Villages, Pouilly-Fuisse, Saint-Veran, Vire-Clesse).
Vodka originally produced at the Mariinsk Distillery in Siberia; international rights sold to Noblewood Group in 2022.
Hitachino Nest beers brewed by Kiuchi Brewery in Naka, Ibaraki Prefecture, founded 1823 as a sake brewery.
Single-estate tequila produced at Destileria La Alteria in Arandas, Los Altos (Highlands) of Jalisco.
Kentucky straight bourbon produced at Wild Turkey Distillery in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky; owned by Campari Group.
Sake brand of Saito Shuzo, located in Fushimi, Kyoto, founded 1895; uses Kyoto rice and Fushimi water.
Brand from Maison Ferrand sourcing rums from multiple Caribbean countries (Barbados, Jamaica, Trinidad, Guyana) with Cognac finishing.
Speyside single malt distillery founded 1896 in the village of Knockando, Moray; sherry-cask matured.
Gypsy/contract brewery founded 2010 in Copenhagen by Jeppe Jarnit-Bjergso; brews collaboratively at multiple breweries internationally.
Flagship brand of Nishiyama Sake Brewery in Tamba City, Hyogo Prefecture, founded 1849.
Family-owned Cognac house in Segonzac, Charente, with 24 hectares planted entirely on Grande Champagne terroir.
Mars Shinshu distillery is in Miyada, Nagano Prefecture, Japan's highest whisky distillery at 800m; owned by Hombo Shuzo.
Sake brewery established 1891 in Yokote, Akita Prefecture, known for snowy winter brewing climate.
Tequila brand from Casa Cuervo; agaves sourced from the Highlands (Los Altos) of Jalisco.
Independent blender (Kilmarnock) recreating closed historic distilleries' styles using blended malts from across Scotland.
Sake brewery established 1865 in Sakaiminato City, Tottori Prefecture, on the Sea of Japan coast.
Cuban cigar brand registered 1865; tobacco from the Vuelta Abajo region (San Juan y Martinez plantation) of Pinar del Rio.
Independent bottler range (Hunter Laing) of single-cask single malts sourced from distilleries across Scotland.
Cognac house founded 1838, sourcing eaux-de-vie from across the Cognac AOC region in Charente; no longer owns vineyards.
Premium 100% blue agave tequila produced in Tequila, Jalisco; estate-grown agaves from El Valle (lowlands).
Loch Lomond Distillery in Alexandria, near Loch Lomond, classified as Highland (sits on the Highland Boundary Fault).
Belgian fruit/sour beer brewery in Oudenaarde, East Flanders, dating to 1679; owned by Duvel Moortgat since 2008.
French blended malt whisky brand sourcing from distilleries in Lille (or Brittany), Alsace and near Cognac, blended/finished in Charente.
Sake brand from Miyaizumi Meijo Brewery in the Aizu region of Fukushima Prefecture, launched 2008.
Artisanal mezcal made in Santiago Matatlan, Oaxaca, from organic Espadin agave; founded 2012 by Ivan Saldana.
American whiskey distiller based in Louisville, Kentucky; main distilling at Shively, with Fort Nelson as the visitor distillery.
Czech brewery in Nachod, Hradec Kralove Region, Bohemia, owned by the city of Nachod; trademark dates to 1935.
Small-batch artisanal mezcal made in San Luis del Rio and San Baltazar Chichicapam in Oaxaca; now owned by Diageo.
Highland coastal single malt distillery in Wick, Caithness, founded 1826 by James Henderson.
Spanish brewery founded 1876 in Barcelona, Catalonia; producer of Estrella Damm; main facilities in Barcelona and El Prat de Llobregat.
Loire estate in Le Landreau, Muscadet-Sevre-et-Maine; certified organic and biodynamic; terroir-driven Melon de Bourgogne.
Colombian rum producer based in Cartagena since 1913; aged spirits company sourcing rums from Caribbean and Panama distilleries.
Belgian witbier brewery in the village of Hoegaarden, Flemish Brabant; revived by Pierre Celis in 1967, now owned by AB InBev.
Sake brewery in Aga, Niigata Prefecture, founded mid-19th century; uses local rice and soft mountain water from Mount Kirin.
World's oldest distilled spirits company, founded 1575 in Amsterdam; produces genever and liqueurs at facilities in the Netherlands.
Diageo brand sourced from three distilleries: Dufftown and Glendullan (Speyside) and Glen Ord (Highland), each marketed to different regions.
Speyside single malt distillery in Dufftown, Moray, founded 1886 by William Grant; world's best-selling single malt.
Sake and liqueur producer in Yoshino, Nara Prefecture, founded 1868; known for floral and fruit-based liqueurs.
Belgian abbey-style brewery in Watou, West Flanders; brewed Westvleteren-style ales under license, then own brand from 1992.
Craft beer brand from Korea Craft Brewery in Eumseong, Chungcheongbuk-do; not a soju producer.
Speyside single malt distillery in the town of Rothes, Moray; founded 1879 and known for sherry-cask matured whiskies.
Independent bottler range (Douglas Laing) of single cask single malts sourced from distilleries across Scotland.
Sake brewery in Echizen Ono, Fukui Prefecture, founded 1751; specializes in ginjo-grade hand-crafted sake.
Islay farm distillery established 2005 near Machir Bay; one of few distilleries practicing traditional floor malting.
Islay farm distillery established 2005 near Machir Bay; produces Islay's only Single Farm Single Malt.
Munich brewery founded 1634 by Paulaner monks; one of the six Oktoberfest breweries, brewed under the Bavarian purity law.
Highland single malt distillery on the Ardnamurchan peninsula, Lochaber, established 2014 by Adelphi.
Kentucky straight bourbon distilled at Wild Turkey Distillery in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky.
Bourbon brand based in Louisville, Kentucky, founded 1997 by Trey Zoeller; owned by Pernod Ricard since 2019.
Highland single malt produced at Knockdhu Distillery in Knock, Banffshire, founded 1893; owned by Inver House.
Highland single malt produced at Knockdhu Distillery in Knock, Banffshire, founded 1893.
Islay single malt distillery on Islay's south coast, founded 1816; owned by Diageo, known for heavily peated 16-year-old.
Belgian Strong Pale Ale produced by Duvel Moortgat in Breendonk, Antwerp Province; brewery founded 1871.
Umeshu producer headquartered in Habikino, Osaka, founded 1914; uses Nanko-ume plums from Wakayama's Kishu area.
Single malt distillery in Carbost, Isle of Skye, founded 1830; the only surviving distillery from Skye's seven historical distilleries.
Speyside single malt distillery near Elgin, Moray, opened 2010 by Diageo; designed to produce multiple spirit characters.
West Highland single malt distillery in Oban, Argyll, founded 1794; one of Scotland's smallest with only two pot stills.
Sake brewery in Asahikawa, Hokkaido, founded 1899; uses Hokkaido rice and underground water with a unique ice-dome brewing method.
Kokubu Sake Brewery in Kirishima City, Kagoshima Prefecture, producing sweet potato shochu from local ingredients.
Cocktail bitters and liqueurs producer founded 2006 in Munich, Bavaria, by bartenders Stephan Berg and Alexander Hauck.
Cognac house from Maison Ferrand in Ars, Charente; produces only Grande Champagne cognacs from 27 hectares.
Mars Shinshu distillery is in Miyada, Nagano Prefecture; owned by Hombo Shuzo. Komagatake is the flagship single malt.
Independent bottler range (Douglas Laing) introduced 2013 featuring single cask single malts and grains from across Scotland.
World's oldest brewery, located in Freising, Bavaria; brewing on Weihenstephan hill since 1040; owned by the Free State of Bavaria.
Cognac house founded 1715 in Cognac; signature focus on Borderies cru but blends use eaux-de-vie from multiple Cognac crus.
Speyside single malt distillery in Craigellachie, Moray, licensed to distil since 1824; owned by Edrington.
Mediterranean gin produced in Vilanova i la Geltru, Catalonia, by the Giro family at MG Distillery; uses Arbequina olives and Mediterranean herbs.
Mezcal artesanal made from 100% Espadin agave in San Juan del Rio, Oaxaca, at the Lopez Sosa family palenque.
Masahiro Distillery in Itoman, Okinawa, founded 1883; produces awamori, Okinawa's traditional rice spirit.
Independent bottler range (Douglas Laing) of single cask single malts sourced from distilleries across Scotland, including Islay.
